name: chronicle-workflow
description: Complete workflow for tracking development work with Chronicle - session recording, git tracking, AI summarization, and Obsidian documentation. Works with CLI commands (portable) or MCP tools (faster). Use when starting a new development session, setting up project tracking, or when user wants comprehensive session management.
capabilities: Guide users through Chronicle session setup and start commands
Explain CLI commands for session listing, timeline, and search
Describe chunked summarization for large sessions
Provide multi-project tracking and filtering patterns
Outline Obsidian integration for knowledge base building
author: ChandlerHardy
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. All 61 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown code formatting (backticks for inline code) as shell command execution, and metadata fields as cryptographic code. The skill provides guidance on using Chronicle CLI commands but does not execute any code, access filesystems, or make network calls.
valueStatement: Development work often gets lost in terminal sessions. Chronicle records everything you do and generates AI summaries so you can find past work, build a knowledge base, and avoid repeating mistakes.
limitations: Only works on Unix systems with script command available
Cannot retroactively track sessions started without Chronicle
Users must manually start sessions with chronicle start command
MCP tools require separate Chronicle MCP server installation

useCases.title: Track Daily Development
useCases.description: Record each coding session and build searchable history of your work over time

useCases.title: Audit Team Progress
useCases.description: Track what the team worked on, link commits to sessions, and review summaries

useCases.title: Build Knowledge Base
useCases.description: Export sessions to Obsidian and create interconnected documentation from past work

promptTemplates.title: Start Session
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to start tracking this development session with Chronicle. Help me set it up correctly.

promptTemplates.title: Check Tracking
promptTemplates.prompt: Am I currently in a Chronicle tracked session? If not, how do I start one?

promptTemplates.title: Review Past Work
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me my Chronicle sessions from yesterday related to [topic] so I can remember what I was working on.

promptTemplates.title: Full Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through the complete Chronicle workflow from starting a session, through development, to documenting to Obsidian.

bestPractices: Start Chronicle sessions early since retroactive tracking is not possible
Make frequent git commits within sessions for better session-commit linking
Document important sessions to Obsidian to build a searchable knowledge base
antiPatterns: Waiting until the end of work to start Chronicle - cannot track retroactively
Skipping commits during sessions - misses valuable tracking context
Never reviewing summaries - defeats the purpose of session documentation

faq.question: What systems does Chronicle support?
faq.answer: Chronicle requires Unix systems with the script command. Not available on Windows without WSL.

faq.question: How large can sessions be?
faq.answer: Sessions of unlimited size work through chunked summarization. Tested with 83,000+ line sessions.

faq.question: Does Chronicle integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: Yes, Obsidian integration via MCP tools and git commit linking within 30-minute windows.

faq.question: Is my data safe with Chronicle?
faq.answer: Yes, local-first storage in ~/.ai-session/ with SQLite. Only AI summaries leave your machine.

faq.question: Why is my session not being tracked?
faq.answer: Chronicle only tracks sessions started explicitly with chronicle start claude or similar command.

faq.question: How does Chronicle compare to manual notes?
faq.answer: Automatic transcript capture, AI summarization, git linking, and searchable history outperform manual notes.
